rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE DOBOSIEWICZ LIVING TRUST UNDER DECLARATION OF TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 29, 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents, And Imposing Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 1, CREDITOR MICHAEL BASSI'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents, And Imposing Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Hearing required. Moving party to bring proposed order to hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NEGINA MOHAMMAD VS. LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Compelling Reponses To Req For Production Of Docs Against
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF NEGINA MOHAMMAD's Motion For Order Compelling Reponses To Request For Production Of Documents. Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Motion to compel further response to plaintiff's document request granted to the extent defendant shall provide a verified response addressing the existence/non-existence of electronic or digital documents and produce same to the extent they exist. Defendant shall produce a witness to testify concerning the record retention policy, the location of the premises where the documents were stored, the facts concerning the water damage to the records, what happened to the damaged records and where the damaged records are currently stored. The motion in all other respects is denied. If further discovery reveals the existence of responsive documents, a more specifically framed document request may be served. Monetary sanctions of $2,238.75 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff Negina Mohammed and against Defendant Anetsberger Brothers, Inc. to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ZIAD SLEIMAN VS. DJAMOL GAFUROV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Claire Fahrbach'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery (For Entry Of The Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CLAIRE FAHRBACH'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery. (For Entry Of The Tentative Ruling Only) (Part 2 of 2.) 30: Ruling: Granted. Defendant to provide code-compliant responses/ responsive documents that show a breakdown of ratings for customer service, efficiency, professionalism, and safety for the driver as of the date of the incident. Objections: Confusing, unintelligible, and argumentative as to the term "driver quality report card.": overruled Relevance: overruled; Privacy rights of nonparties: overruled; Overbroad as to time and scope/less burdensome means: overruled. 33: Ruling: Denied. Defendant has offered to provide GPS data from its App reflecting the trip request in progress of the time the subject accident if plaintiff signs a protective order. If plaintiff wants this information, she must sign a protective order. Objections: Vague and ambiguous: overruled; Overbroad as to scope/relevance: sustained; Confidential and proprietary information: overruled; Privacy rights of nonparties: overruled; Unduly burdensome and oppressive: sustained. 39: Ruling: Denied. The request is overbroad. Objections: Vague ambiguous and overbroad: overruled; Overbroad in scope/relevance: sustained. 49: Ruling: Denied. The request is overbroad. Objections: Vague and ambiguous: overruled; Fails to identify with sufficient particularity: sustained; Overbroad as to time and scope/relevance: sustained; Confidential and proprietary information: sustained. 50: Ruling: Denied. The request is overbroad. Objections: Vague, ambiguous and confusing: overruled; Fails to identify with sufficient particularity: sustained; Overbroad as to time and scope/relevance: sustained; Unduly burdensome and harassing: sustained; Confidential and proprietary information: sustained; Privacy rights: sustained. 51: Ruling: Denied. The request is overbroad. Objections: Vague, ambiguous and confusing: overruled; Fails to identify with sufficient particularity the documents sought: sustained; Over broad as to time and scope/relevance: sustained; Unduly burdensome and harassing: sustained; Confidential and proprietary information: sustained; Privacy rights of nonparties: overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ZIAD SLEIMAN VS. DJAMOL GAFUROV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Disco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CLAIRE FAHRBACH'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalonotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Motion is Granted and Denied in part. Meet and Confer: The Court finds that the meet and confer efforts were sufficient. Sanctions: Denied. The parties are required to meet and confer regarding a protective order before the hearing. The parties shall bring a an agreed-to form of protective order to the hearing. Amended Responses and responsive documents to be provided within 15 days of Notice of Entry of this order. Defendant to prepare a proposed order consistent with this ruling. Special Interrogatories: 41: Ruling: Granted. Defendant to provide code compliant responses. Defendant may redact private personal information of nonparties. Objections: Discovery not yet completed: overruled; Work product, legal analysis, and theories: overruled; Invade the privacy rights of nonparties: overruled. 102: Ruling: Denied. Defendant responded to this interrogatory. Objections: Vague, ambiguous and confusing: overruled; Overbroad: overruled; reasonably tailored/not reasonably calculated: overruled; Equally available: overruled. 103: Ruling: Denied. This interrogatory is overbroad. Objections: Vague, ambiguous and confusing: overruled; Overbroad as to time and scope: sustained; burdensome, harassing/not reasonably tailored: sustained. (Continued to part 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER RODRIGUES et al VS. PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIOA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTs PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC., SCOTT REAY, JON-MICHAEL JOHNSON and CHUCK BONECK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Defendants fail to show that the DFEH charges are defective as a matter of law. "Administrative charges are to be construed liberally because they are often drafted by claimants without the assistance of counsel. Accordingly, '[i]t is sufficient that the EEOC be apprised, in general terms, of the alleged discriminatory parties and the alleged discriminatory acts.'" Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 266-267. In this case, the charges meet the requirements of Cal. Gov't Code sec. 12960 and 2 CCR sec. 10005. The demurrer is moot as to Mr. Boneck. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: CRAIG K MARTIN VS. DOES 1-50, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT ANTHONY LINCOLN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. The Complaint fails to allege any conduct by Defendant to support any of the causes of action asserted against him. The Complaint also fails to allege any facts to support the formation or existence of a conspiracy between Defendant and any other named Defendant. Further, Plaintiff bases Defendant's liability as a coconspirator on an erroneous legal basis-i.e., that Defendant became a member of the alleged conspiracy by filing a complaint against Plaintiff with the State Bar. Thus, the Complaint cannot be cured by amendment because such conduct could not form nor further any such alleged conspiracy. Moreover, with respect to any claims resulting from alleged violations of the Ralph Act (Civ. Code § 51.7) or the Bane Act (Civ. Code § 52.1), Plaintiff fails to allege that any violent acts or threatened violent acts were committed by anyone, let alone the moving party. Plaintiff alleges verbal abuse by another named defendant but this is not sufficient to state a constitutional deprivation of rights per Title 42 U.S.C. § 1983. (Oltarzewski v. Ruggiero (9th Cir. 1987) 830 F.2d 136, 139.) Nor are any facts alleged linking the moving party to this conduct other than the bare conclusion, without more, that they are conspirators. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JKS)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG K MARTIN VS. DOES 1-50, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT MICHAEL KECK's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an anti-SLAPP motion under CCP § 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity. . . . If the court finds such a showing has been made . . . it then determines whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) In determining whether the allegations arise from a protected activity, the court's "focus is on the principal thrust or gravamen of the causes of action, i.e., the allegedly wrongful and injury producing conduct that provides the foundation for the claims." (Castleman v. Sagaser (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 481, 490-91.) Here, each of Plaintiff's causes of action arises out of the State Bar disciplinary proceedings brought against him or the defense of the federal suit filed by Plaintiff. Thus, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute is met. Plaintiff has not demonstrated a probability of prevailing. A plaintiff is required to "show both that the claim is legally sufficient and there is admissible evidence that, if credited, would be sufficient to sustain a favorable judgment." (McGarry v. University of San Diego 154 Cal.App.4th 97, 108.) Plaintiff here failed to present any evidence in opposition, and therefore failed to demonstrate a probability of prevailing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JKS)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JKS)

rulings.Case Title: DERRICK A. RAMIREZ VS. MICHEAL D. ZOOK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, July 31, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT MICHEAL ZOOK's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action. Plaintiff has 15 days leave to amend to allege the cause of action with more particularity. The court rejects defendant's remaining arguments. Even if the court takes judicial notice of the documents that defendant presented, those documents do not show that the parties did not have a partnership. In addition, the complaint and documents do not show that plaintiff's claims are time barred. Paragraph 10 of the complaint alleges that the breach occurred in 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JKS)
